data.title: Binance to Conduct Ethereum Network Wallet Maintenance
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to perform wallet maintenance for the Ethereum Network (ETH) on 2026-04-07 at 06:00 (UTC). To facilitate this maintenance, deposits and withdrawals on the Ethereum Network will be temporarily suspended starting from 2026-04-07 at 05:55 (UTC). The maintenance is expected to last approximately one hour.

During this period, the trading of tokens on the Ethereum Network will remain unaffected. Binance assures users that all technical requirements will be managed by the platform, ensuring a smooth process. Once the network upgrade is confirmed to be stable, deposits and withdrawals for tokens on the Ethereum Network will be reopened. No additional announcements will be made regarding the resumption of these services.

data.title: Market news: Saudi Arabia raises oil prices to a record premium of $19.50 per barrel.
data.description: Market news: Saudi Arabia has raised its oil price premium to a record $19.50 per barrel, with prices expected to reach $40 per barrel. (Jinshi)

data.title: BIT: Ethereum call option selling pressure rises, volatility continues to decline.
data.description: BIT released a chart today stating that market trading volume has shrunk significantly, lacking a clear directional catalyst. While the Bitcoin ETF recorded a net inflow of approximately $1.3 billion, Ethereum continued to experience capital outflows, further exacerbating the demand divergence between the two. From a macro perspective, despite lingering geopolitical risks, volatility continues to converge, making put options strategies increasingly favored. Recent changes in the Ethereum options market are particularly noteworthy: the proportion of selling upside call options has increased dramatically, indicating a lack of confidence in a short-term ETH rebound. A few months ago, traders were actively buying upside exposure; now the tide has turned, with more Ethereum traders choosing to earn premiums by selling call options rather than betting on a directional upward move.

data.title: Web3 and AI Integration Presents Opportunities for Development
data.description: Teams capable of navigating both Web3 and AI dimensions are currently facing a prime opportunity for involvement. According to PANews, there is significant potential in building more reliable on-chain agent systems at the execution level and bridging data, permissions, and trust at the infrastructure level. These areas present considerable gaps that need to be addressed.

data.title: Hyperliquid's Weekend Price Discovery Shows Improvement
data.description: Research indicates that Hyperliquid's crude oil contract depth was significantly lower than CME's during overlapping weekday hours, with figures showing $152,000 compared to CME's $19 million within a plus or minus 2 basis point range. According to NS3.AI, the study also revealed that Hyperliquid's weekend price discovery improved over three weekends, eventually becoming a reliable indicator for predicting the direction of CME's Monday opening by the third weekend.

data.title: Bitcoin's Potential Return to $70,000 This Month: Polymarket Data Insights
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Polymarket data indicates a 91% probability that Bitcoin will reach $70,000 this month. Additionally, there is a 47% chance that Bitcoin could hit $75,000. These predictions reflect market sentiment and expectations surrounding Bitcoin's price movements.

data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 5.97 million ATSER tokens from Binance, worth $3.99 million, and may have transferred them to Aster.

data.title: Cosmos Hub Proposal Update: Osmosis DEX Adjusts ATOM Acquisition Strategy
data.description:
Cosmos ecosystem decentralized exchange (DEX) Osmosis has announced an update to its Cosmos Hub proposal based on feedback from validators and the community. According to Foresight News, the most significant change is the decision to cease minting new ATOM tokens. Instead, the required ATOM will be gradually purchased on the open market using revenue from the Osmosis DEX protocol for OSMO/ATOM conversions. The total acquisition will remain capped at less than 2.5% of the total ATOM supply. The proposal is currently in the governance discussion phase within the Cosmos Hub.

Previously, Foresight News reported that Osmosis had proposed a significant initiative to the Cosmos ecosystem, aiming to integrate Osmosis directly into the Cosmos Hub. If governance approvals are obtained from both chains, Osmosis DEX will natively migrate to the Hub, thereby unifying liquidity, governance, and security on a single chain.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Thailand's Gem and Jewelry Exports Decline in February 2026
data.description: According to Jin10, Thailand's gem and jewelry exports, excluding gold, amounted to $1.62404 billion in February 2026, marking a 29.32% decrease compared to the same period last year. This decline reversed the growth observed in January. When including gold, the total export value reached $2.72777 billion, a decrease of 15.59%. For the first two months of 2026, exports excluding gold totaled $3.43216 billion, down 14.88%, while the total including gold rose by 18.92% to $7.29397 billion. Gold remained the standout category, with February exports alone reaching $1.10374 billion, an 18.22% increase, marking the seventh consecutive month of growth. Factors driving the increase in gold exports include rising international gold prices, geopolitical tensions between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, new tariffs imposed by the U.S. under Section 122, and continued purchases by the SPDR Gold Trust. In the first two months of the year, gold exports totaled $3.86181 billion, an 83.76% increase.

data.title: Oil's 12-Month Rate of Change Nears Critical Level Amid Market Concerns
data.description: Oil's 12-month rate of change has reached 91%, approaching the 100% threshold that analysts have identified as a precursor to previous market crashes. According to NS3.AI, Brent crude prices rose above $111 per barrel on Monday, raising concerns about potential market instability. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments, as historical patterns suggest that such rapid increases in oil prices could lead to broader economic repercussions.

data.title: Polymarket's probability of Bitcoin rebounding to $70,000 in April has surged to 91%, a 19% increase in the last 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Bitcoin rising to $70,000 in April has surged to 91% on Polymarket, a 19% increase in 24 hours; the probability of Bitcoin rising to $75,000 in April has risen to 47%, a 16% increase in 24 hours. The event contract rules state that if, within the specified month (from 00:00 AM EST on the first day to 11:59 PM EST on the last day), the highest price of BTC/USDT on Binance exchange is equal to or higher than the specified price on any 1-minute candlestick chart, the market will immediately declare "yes"; otherwise, the market will declare "no." As of now, the total trading volume of this event contract has exceeded $6.435 million. Market data shows that BTC is currently trading at 69,164.6 USDT, up 3.06% in the last 24 hours. The total amount of BTC liquidations across the network in the past 24 hours reached $113 million, including $104 million in short positions and $8.41 million in long positions. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Criticizes Supreme Court's Decisions
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump expressed disappointment over the Supreme Court's inability to watch Mark Levin's show discussing the 'birthright citizenship scam.' According to Odaily, Trump believes that if the justices had seen the program, they would not allow the alleged scam to continue. He urged the court to use common sense for the nation's benefit, criticizing their handling of tariff issues, which he claims resulted in unnecessary losses of billions in potential tax refunds to appease detractors and fraudsters. Trump warned against repeating past mistakes, emphasizing that the country cannot afford poor decisions from a seemingly indifferent court.

data.title: Bank of Japan Warns of Potential Supply Disruptions Due to Rising Energy Prices
data.description: The Bank of Japan has expressed concerns that rising energy prices and increasing inflation could lead to broader supply disruptions. According to Jin10, these economic challenges may negatively impact the confidence of households and businesses across certain regions. The central bank's warning highlights the potential for economic instability as energy costs continue to climb, posing risks to both supply chains and consumer sentiment.

data.title: Trump angrily denounces the Supreme Court: "A series of terrible decisions" on tariffs and birthright citizenship.
data.description: Trump: It's a shame the Supreme Court can't watch the Mark Levin Show about the birthright citizenship scam tonight. If they had, they would never have let that money-grabbing scam continue. They should use their common sense for the good of our country. They've already lost badly on tariffs, unnecessarily costing the U.S. hundreds of billions of dollars in potential tax refunds to cater to haters and fraudsters. Why? Don't repeat the same mistakes! This country can't afford any more bad decisions from a seemingly indifferent court.

data.title: Bitcoin's Profit-to-Loss Transaction Ratio Reaches 2.95:1
data.description: Bitcoin's transaction ratio of profits to losses reached 2.95:1 last weekend, according to NS3.AI. This metric evaluates the price of Bitcoin when it is moved compared to its acquisition price, indicating a significant number of transactions were profitable during this period.

data.title: US-Iran Conflict May De-escalate by Late April, Says South Korean Intelligence
data.description: South Korea's National Intelligence Service (NIS) has indicated that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran could enter a de-escalation phase by the end of April. According to NS3.AI, the NIS highlighted that the next three to four days are critical in determining the trajectory of the conflict. The United States has reportedly achieved tactical military successes; however, it faces challenges in securing political concessions from Iran. Additionally, the NIS noted that Iran is leveraging its influence over global energy supply chains while considering its response to U.S. demands concerning its nuclear program.

data.title: India's March Services PMI Final Value Exceeds Expectations
data.description: India's services sector showed stronger growth in March, with the Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) reaching a final value of 57.5, surpassing the expected 57.4. According to Jin10, this figure also marks an increase from the previous value of 57.2, indicating continued expansion in the services industry.

data.title: The Israeli military claims to have launched airstrikes on infrastructure in Tehran, Iran.
data.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) stated on April 6 that the Israeli Air Force had recently completed a series of airstrikes against infrastructure in Tehran. Earlier, Iranian sources reported that a residential area in eastern Tehran was attacked early that morning, resulting in 4 deaths, 7 injuries, the complete destruction of 3 residential buildings, and severe damage to 50 other structures. (CCTV News)

data.title: Shell's Share Price Rises Amid Venezuelan Gas Negotiations
data.description: Shell's shares have increased by over 27% since January 2026. According to NS3.AI, this rise is linked to Shell's ongoing negotiations to access approximately 20 trillion cubic feet of Venezuelan gas for the Atlantic LNG project in Trinidad. The company has identified the nearby Loran field as a promising source due to its potential connection to the Manatee infrastructure in Trinidad. However, the involvement of Russia's state enterprise Roszarubezhneft, which holds certain rights, continues to complicate the deal.
